selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a clear understanding of the reasons why people create and share false information. These can include seeking attention, manipulating public opinion, or simply having fun. Once you understand the motivations behind fake content, you can take steps to identify and address it more effectively. This may involve using fact-checking tools, monitoring your own social media accounts regularly, and being mindful of the sources of information you consume. Additionally, promoting critical thinking and media literacy education can help create a more informed and discerning public, better equipped to distinguish between reliable and unreliable sources of information.",
"reasoning_summary": "To identify and address fake content on social media, it is essential to understand the reasons why people create and share false information. By recognizing these motivations and taking steps to verify the accuracy of information, individuals can better navigate the complex online landscape and avoid being misled by false or misleading information.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": []
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a clear understanding of the reasons why people create and share false information. These can include seeking attention, manipulating public opinion, or simply having fun. Once you understand the motivations behind fake content, you can take steps to identify and address it more effectively. This may involve using fact-checking tools, monitoring your own social media accounts regularly, and being mindful of the sources of information you consume. Additionally, promoting critical thinking and media literacy education can help create a more informed and discerning public, better equipped to distinguish between reliable and unreliable sources of information.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To identify and address fake content on social media, it is essential to understand the reasons why people create and share false information. By recognizing these motivations and taking steps to verify the accuracy of information, individuals can better navigate the complex online landscape and avoid being misled by false or misleading information.
